description:
A conceptual design team needs to estimate the internal fuel capacity of the eCRM-001 transport aircraft. Open the baseline model at /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/eCRM-001_wing_tail.vsp3 in OpenVSP. Select the main 'Wing' component, navigate to its Subsurface ('Sub') tab, and add a new Subsurface. Set its type to 'Tank'. Configure the spanwise boundaries (U) to start at 0.15 and end at 0.70. Configure the chordwise boundaries (W) to start at 0.20 and end at 0.65. Once the tank geometry is defined, run an analysis (such as CompGeom or Mass Properties) to compute the total theoretical internal volume of this tank. Save the modified model as /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/eCRM001_wet_wing.vsp3. Finally, write a text report to /home/ga/Desktop/fuel_capacity_report.txt that includes the extracted Tank Volume (in m^3) and the calculated Total Fuel Mass (in kg). Assume a Jet-A fuel density of 804 kg/m^3. Format the report exactly as follows:

Fuel Capacity Report
====================
Tank Volume: XX.X m^3
Total Fuel Mass: XXXX.X kg
